hints.Only 'Vivamus': Both 'Vivamus' and 'amemus' end in -mus, the 1st person plural verb ending
hints.Only 'amemus': Both 'Vivamus' and 'amemus' end in -mus, the 1st person plural verb ending
hints.'atque' and 'amemus': 'atque' is a conjunction meaning 'and' — both 'Vivamus' and 'amemus' are verbs

hints.Indicative: Indicative present 1st pl. would be 'vivimus' — 'Vivamus' has the -a- subjunctive marker
hints.Imperative: Imperative of 'vivere' would be 'vive' (sg.) or 'vivite' (pl.) — 'Vivamus' is 1st person subjunctive
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive of 'vivere' is 'vivere' — 'Vivamus' is a conjugated form

hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are the principal parts of 'vincere' (to conquer)
hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the principal parts of 'videre' (to see)
hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the principal parts of 'venire' (to come)

hints.Indicative: Indicative present 1st pl. would be 'amamus' — 'amemus' has the -e- subjunctive vowel change
hints.Imperative: Imperative of 'amare' would be 'ama' (sg.) or 'amate' (pl.) — 'amemus' is 1st person subjunctive
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive of 'amare' is 'amare' — 'amemus' is a conjugated form

hints.habeo, habere, habui, habitum: These are the principal parts of 'habere' (to have)
hints.laudo, laudare, laudavi, laudatum: These are the principal parts of 'laudare' (to praise)
hints.paro, parare, paravi, paratum: These are the principal parts of 'parare' (to prepare)

hints.Adverb: Adverbs modify verbs — 'atque' joins the two subjunctive verbs together
hints.Preposition: Prepositions govern nouns — 'atque' connects two verbs as a coordinating conjunction
hints.Interjection: Interjections express emotion — 'atque' is a conjunction meaning 'and'

translation: Let us live and let us love
